questions.question: When an object is placed at the center of curvature (C) of a concave mirror, what is the nature of the image formed?
questions.option_2: Highly diminished, point-sized
questions.option_3: Virtual and erect
questions.solution: When an object is placed at the centre of curvature (C) of a concave mirror, the image forms at the centre of curvature itself. The image is real because the light rays actually converge at this point. It is inverted as the rays cross each other. The size of the image is equal to the size of the object. Hence, the correct nature of the image is: real and inverted. Final answer: Option 1

questions.question: When using the sign convention for spherical mirrors, which direction along the principal axis is considered positive?
questions.option_2: Upwards from the principal axis
questions.option_3: To the left of the origin (along - x-axis)
questions.option_4: To the right of the origin (along + x-axis)
questions.solution: Solution: In spherical mirrors, direction of incident light is taken as positive Light travels from left to right Hence, distances measured to the right are positive Answer: To the right of the origin (along + x-axis)

questions.question: Which material can be magnetised using the strong magnetic field inside a solenoid?
questions.option_2: Plastic
questions.option_3: Copper
questions.solution: Soft iron can be magnetised by placing it inside a strong magnetic field such as inside a solenoid. This is because soft iron is a ferromagnetic material that easily gains magnetic properties when subjected to an external magnetic field. Materials like aluminum, plastic, and copper are not easily magnetised as they are paramagnetic or diamagnetic and do not retain magnetism. Therefore, the correct choice is soft iron. Final answer: Option 4

questions.question: Which measurable quantity remains the same when a liquid is transferred between containers?
questions.option_1: Mass of the liquid becomes zero
questions.option_2: Density of the liquid always increases
questions.option_3: Volume of the liquid remains unchanged
questions.option_4: Shape of the liquid remains unchanged
questions.solution: The volume of a liquid is a measure of the space it occupies. When transferring a liquid from one container to another, no liquid is lost, so its volume remains the same. Mass may remain same but does not become zero; density can change with temperature; shape changes according to container. Hence, the measurable quantity that remains unchanged is the volume of the liquid. Final answer: Option 3

questions.question: Which of the following arrangements produces a nearly uniform magnetic field in the central region of a circular current-carrying system under steady conditions?
questions.option_2: Allowing current to vary with time in a single loop
questions.option_3: Using multiple closely wound turns of identical radius with constant current
questions.solution: The most uniform magnetic field in the central region is produced when multiple loops are arranged. Specifically, multiple closely wound turns of identical radius create nearly uniform magnetic fields by adding their effects. Random orientations or varying radii distort the uniformity of the field. Time-varying current in a single loop does not produce a steady uniform magnetic field. Therefore, using multiple closely wound turns with constant current is the best choice for uniformity. Final answer: Option 3

questions.question: Which of the following does NOT define the motion of a pendulum swinging back and forth?
questions.option_2: The pendulum exhibits periodic motion
questions.option_3: The pendulum exhibits non-uniform motion
questions.solution: The motion of a pendulum is not uniform because its speed varies during the swinging. A pendulum swinging back and forth exhibits periodic motion, meaning it repeats at regular intervals. The motion is non-uniform due to changing speed, so it exhibits non-uniform motion. It also exhibits accelerated motion as acceleration changes direction during the swing. Hence, the pendulum does NOT exhibit uniform motion. Final answer: Option 1

questions.question: Which of the following is usually 'stored under high pressure'?
questions.option_2: Solid CO2
questions.option_3: N2
questions.solution: Option 1 is H2 (Hydrogen). Hydrogen gas is typically stored under high pressure in cylinders because it is highly flammable and storing it compressed reduces the volume significantly. Solid CO2 (dry ice) is stored at low temperatures, not under high pressure. Nitrogen (N2) is also stored under pressure but usually less commonly referenced than hydrogen in this context. NaCl is a solid and does not require storage under high pressure. Hence, the common gas stored under high pressure here is hydrogen (H2). Final answer: Option 1

questions.question: Which of the following situations is NOT an example of action and reaction forces?
questions.option_2: A bat hitting a cricket ball
questions.option_3: A ball thrown upward comes back down due to the pull of gravity.
questions.solution: Option 3 is correct because the ball coming down due to gravity is not an action-reaction pair. It is a single force acting on the ball (gravity). Action and reaction forces always act in pairs on two different bodies, equal in magnitude and opposite in direction. Examples: 1. Swimmer pushes water backwards (action), water pushes swimmer forward (reaction). 2. Bat hits ball (action), ball exerts equal opposite force on bat (reaction). 4. Rocket expels gas downwards (action), gas pushes rocket upwards (reaction). Thus, option 3 is NOT an example of action and reaction forces. Final answer: Option 3

questions.question: Which of the following will diffuse slowest in air?
questions.option_2: Chlorine
questions.option_3: Ammonia
questions.solution: Diffusion rate is inversely proportional to the square root of molar mass (Graham's law). Molar masses: Oxygen (32 g/mol), Chlorine (around 71 g/mol), Ammonia (17 g/mol), Hydrogen (2 g/mol). Chlorine has the highest molar mass among these gases. Therefore, chlorine diffuses slowest in air due to its greater molar mass. Answer is Option 2.

questions.question: Which property correctly distinguishes liquids from solids?
questions.option_1: Liquids have fixed shape but no fixed volume
questions.option_2: Liquids have fixed volume but no fixed shape
questions.option_3: Liquids are completely incompressible in nature
questions.option_4: Liquids cannot diffuse into other substances
questions.solution: Option 2 is correct. Liquids have a fixed volume but no fixed shape. This means they take the shape of the container they are in, unlike solids which have fixed shape and volume. Liquids are not completely incompressible, and they can diffuse, so options 3 and 4 are incorrect. Option 1 is also wrong because liquids don't have a fixed shape. Hence, the key property that distinguishes liquids from solids is that liquids have fixed volume but no fixed shape. Answer ends here.

questions.question: Which property explains why the path of light is not visible when a beam passes through a true solution?
questions.option_1: Particles can be removed by filtration
questions.option_2: Particles are smaller than one nanometre
questions.option_3: Particles settle down on standing still
questions.option_4: Particles are heavier than solvent molecules
questions.solution: The path of light is not visible in a true solution because the particles in it are extremely small. In a true solution, particles are smaller than one nanometre in size. Such tiny particles do not scatter light, so the beam of light remains invisible when passing through the solution. The Tyndall effect, which makes light visible, occurs only if the particles are larger, like in colloids. Hence, the reason is the very small size of particles in a true solution. Final answer: Option 2

questions.question: Which rule helps to determine the direction of force acting on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field?
questions.option_2: Faraday's law
questions.option_3: Ampere's rule
questions.solution: The question asks which rule determines the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field. Fleming's left-hand rule is used to find the direction of force on the conductor. In this rule, the thumb indicates the direction of force, the forefinger the magnetic field, and the middle finger the current. Other options like Faraday's law relate to electromagnetic induction, Ampere's rule is for magnetic field direction, and the right-hand rule is commonly used for different magnetic phenomena. Hence, Fleming's left-hand rule is the correct choice. Final answer: Option 1

questions.question: Which statement accurately describes how energy transforms during the free fall of an object under gravity?
questions.option_1: Potential energy and kinetic energy both decrease.
questions.option_2: Potential energy is converted into heat energy during the fall.
questions.option_3: Both potential and kinetic energies remain constant.
questions.option_4: Potential energy decreases while kinetic energy increases, maintaining constant total mechanical energy.
questions.solution: Option 4 is correct. During free fall under gravity, an object's potential energy decreases as it moves downwards. This lost potential energy converts into kinetic energy, increasing the object's speed. The total mechanical energy (potential + kinetic) remains constant if we ignore air resistance. Hence, the energy transforms from potential to kinetic, conserving total mechanical energy throughout the fall. This is a direct consequence of the law of conservation of energy in a gravitational field. Final answer: Option 4

questions.question: Which type of spherical mirror has its reflecting surface curved inwards, facing towards the centre of the sphere?
questions.option_2: Parabolic mirror
questions.option_3: Convex mirror
questions.solution: Correct option: 4 A spherical mirror where the reflecting surface curves inwards, towards the centre of the sphere, is called a concave mirror. Concave mirrors converge light rays to a focal point. This inward curvature distinguishes it from convex mirrors, whose reflecting surface bulges outwards. Hence, the mirror described is a concave mirror. Final answer: Option 4

questions.question: Why are bulbs filled with nitrogen and argon gases instead of air?
questions.option_1: To prolong the life of the filament
questions.option_2: To increase brightness
questions.option_3: To make the bulb heavier
questions.solution: Bulbs are filled with nitrogen and argon gases to prevent the filament from oxidizing. Air contains oxygen which would cause the filament to burn quickly. Nitrogen and argon being inert gases do not react with the filament. This helps to prolong the life of the filament inside the bulb. Final answer: Option 1

questions.question: Why are solids described as rigid in nature?
questions.option_1: Their particles possess very low mass and volume
questions.option_2: Their particles are closely packed with strong attraction
questions.option_3: Their particles slide easily under applied external force
questions.option_4: Their particles move freely with weak mutual attraction
questions.solution: Solids are called rigid because their particles are very closely packed. This close packing results in strong attractive forces between particles. Strong attraction keeps the particles fixed in position, limiting movement. Due to this, solids maintain a definite shape and resist deformation. Hence, solids appear rigid under external forces. Final answer: Option 2

questions.question: Why do gases exert pressure on the walls of the container?
questions.option_1: Gas particles attract the container walls strongly
questions.option_2: Gas particles move randomly and strike the walls
questions.option_3: Gas particles stay fixed inside the container
questions.option_4: Gas particles possess very low kinetic energy
questions.solution: Correct Option: 2 Gases consist of particles moving randomly in all directions. These particles collide with the walls of the container. Each collision exerts a force on the wall. The continuous collisions of gas particles cause pressure on the container walls. Thus, gas pressure originates from particles striking the walls due to their random motion. This explains why gases exert pressure on container walls.

questions.question: Why do wet clothes dry faster when spread out?
questions.option_2: Water escapes faster due to sunlight
questions.option_3: Water boils off
questions.solution: Correct option is 4 Wet clothes dry because water evaporates from them. When clothes are spread out, their surface area increases. More surface area allows more water molecules to evaporate at the same time. This speeds up the drying process. Hence, wet clothes dry faster when spread out due to increased surface area. This is a simple principle of evaporation rate and surface exposure. Answer is option 4. End of answer.

questions.question: Why does a torch bulb glow only when the switch is turned on?
questions.option_1: Because the bulb is in series with the cell
questions.option_2: Because the bulb is functional
questions.option_3: Because the switch completes the circuit, allowing current to flow
questions.solution: The bulb in a torch glows only when the switch is turned on because the switch completes the electrical circuit. When the circuit is complete, current can flow from the battery through the bulb. If the switch is off, the circuit is open and current cannot flow, so the bulb does not glow. Hence, the switch controls whether the current flows or not by completing or breaking the circuit. Final answer: Option 3

questions.question: Why does our palm feel cold when acetone or petrol is poured on it?
questions.option_1: The liquid increases moisture on skin surface
questions.option_2: The liquid reacts chemically with our skin surface
questions.option_3: The liquid blocks heat flow from surroundings
questions.option_4: The liquid absorbs heat from the skin to evaporate
questions.solution: The palm feels cold because acetone or petrol evaporates quickly. Evaporation requires heat energy, which is taken from the skin’s surface. This heat absorption reduces skin temperature, causing a cool sensation. Hence, the liquid absorbs heat from the skin to evaporate, making it feel cold. Final answer: Option 4

questions.question: Why does the temperature remain constant during melting of ice even on heating?
questions.option_1: Heat energy increases kinetic energy instantly
questions.option_2: Heat energy escapes from ice into the surroundings
questions.option_3: Heat energy converts directly into work
questions.option_4: Heat energy is used to overcome particle attraction
questions.solution: During melting, temperature remains stable because all heat energy supplied is used to break the forces of attraction between particles in ice. This energy required to break these forces is called latent heat of fusion. Hence, temperature does not increase until the entire ice melts. Therefore, heat energy is used to overcome particle attraction during melting. Final answer: Option 4
